channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Iran closes Strait of Hormuz again, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire tenuously holds
item.description: Iran's military says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, while a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is strained, but still in place.
item.content:encoded: Iran's military says it has closed the Strait of Hormuz again, while a ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon is strained, but still in place.
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Tensions between President Trump and Pope Leo put American Catholics in awkward spot
item.description: The divide between the White House and the Vatican is widening after President Trump sharply criticized Pope Leo and the leader of the Catholic Church said he doesn't fear Trump.
item.content:encoded: The divide between the White House and the Vatican is widening after President Trump sharply criticized Pope Leo and the leader of the Catholic Church said he doesn't fear Trump.
item.dc:creator: Jack Morgan

item.title: Newly approved mining in Minnesota may threaten waterways of a beloved nature preserve
item.description: Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota is filled with more than a thousand lakes and rivers. But a recent vote in congress to allow mining nearby could threaten the preserve's waterways.
item.content:encoded: Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness in Minnesota is filled with more than a thousand lakes and rivers. But a recent vote in congress to allow mining nearby could threaten the preserve's waterways.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Mei Semones' genre-bending, bilingual music is unlike anything you've ever heard
item.description: Mei Semones' unique sound weaves lyrics in English and Japanese through catchy pop, jazz and rock-influenced instrumentation.
item.content:encoded: Mei Semones' unique sound weaves lyrics in English and Japanese through catchy pop, jazz and rock-influenced instrumentation.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Ofman

item.title: Amid wars and soaring energy prices, IMF says the global economy is on the brink of recession
item.description: A report from the International Monetary Fund warns "the global economy is threatened with being thrown off course." European Union Commissioner Marta Kos discusses this grim economic outlook.
item.content:encoded: A report from the International Monetary Fund warns "the global economy is threatened with being thrown off course." European Union Commissioner Marta Kos discusses this grim economic outlook.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: What Viktor Orbán's loss means for the future of global populism
item.description: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Anne Applebaum of The Atlantic about what Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's electoral loss means for the future of right-wing populist movements around the world.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Rob Schmitz speaks with Anne Applebaum of The Atlantic about what Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán's electoral loss means for the future of right-wing populist movements around the world.
item.dc:creator: Rob Schmitz

item.title: Reporting on the policies that impact affordability for Americans
item.description: With affordability a top issue for more and more Americans, NPR's Jennifer Ludden talks about her reporting on the policies that impact access to housing and food.
item.content:encoded: With affordability a top issue for more and more Americans, NPR's Jennifer Ludden talks about her reporting on the policies that impact access to housing and food.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Animal activists celebrate their first global 'Sanctuary Day'
item.description: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.content:encoded: It's been 40 years since animal advocates founded a sanctuary for farm animals in New York and California, and they say April 17 is their first global sanctuary day.
item.dc:creator: Abigail Bottar

item.title: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas on his viral court celebrations
item.description: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas doesn't hide his true self, even when he celebrates on the court.
item.content:encoded: Volleyball player Jordan Lucas doesn't hide his true self, even when he celebrates on the court.
item.dc:creator: Jordan-Marie Smith

item.title: Where art thou?: Mapping Shakespeare's 1613 neighborhood
item.description: The recent discovery of a 17th century map provides new insight into the life of William Shakespeare.
item.content:encoded: The recent discovery of a 17th century map provides new insight into the life of William Shakespeare.
item.dc:creator: Erika Ryan
